OatFi Portfolio Review

OatFi — Portfolio Review

As of May 18, 2026  ·  analytics_development.mart  ·  DPD: cutoffdate − mat_dt, curr_upb > 0
Generated 2026-05-18 11:55
Excl: Balance Payments, Oat Financial LLC
Assessment
Claude · auto-generated

# Portfolio Credit Quality Executive Narrative

Portfolio delinquency improved modestly quarter-over-quarter outside of Relay. Order's 30–60 DPD contracted 100 basis points to 18%, while its 60–90 DPD remained elevated at 44%. Koverly advanced $4.95M in loans from the 30–60 to 90–120 bucket, suggesting seasoning rather than acute deterioration. Lawtrades exhibits structural payment timing misalignment across all delinquency bands. Tab Commerce and BRKR.IO showed new stress in the 30–60 and 90–120 buckets respectively. Cledara and NurseIO remained clean at current maturities.

Order carries the largest leading exposure at $10.7M across obligors still within partner due-date windows, concentrated in Retail and Food & Accommodation names including Trendly, Vacatia, and Naturae. BRKR.IO holds $325M in leading balances against only $8K confirmed, a 39:1 ratio signaling near-term payment cliff risk from Venus Brands (26 days past partner due date). Lawtrades' $958K leading balance is distributed across Ironclad and Ripple, with obligor delinquencies already 3–16 days past partner due date. Tab Commerce and NurseIO each show leading balances under $180K. Cledara's $87K leading tranche carries zero confirmed balance and no delinquency history.

Construction sector stress is pronounced at 12.97% DPD 31+, driven primarily by Relay's embedded exposure and independent of broader economy signals. Admin/Support hits 16.84% DPD 31+, a partner-driven dynamic concentrated in Relay's servicer base. Professional Services and Retail each show 6.8–7.1% delinquency rates, spanning multiple partners. Real Estate moved to 11.13%, reflecting deterioration in Flychain's assisted-living book. Health Care at 3.77% and Information at 2.99% remain benign. Wholesale Trade and Food & Accommodation are mid-range at 4.1–4.9%.

Largest 30+ DPD balances are led by HPI Canna Inc. at Order ($210K), Fred's Plumbing at Relay ($189K), and Marino Ave Inc. at Koverly ($185K). KKG Enterprise at Tab Commerce carries $165K in delinquency. Relay dominates the 31+ DPD concentration with Fred's Plumbing, Bethlehem Apparatus, Victory Doors, and Alliance Commercial Cleaning each exceeding $100K. Flychain's two assisted-living facilities—Noble House and Camelot Chateau—each at exactly $100K delinquency suggest facility-level distress rather than servicer issue. Leading obligors show Order concentrated in cannabis, agriculture, and consumer services; Lawtrades' exposure is skewed to Ironclad ($300K+ across three tranches) and Ripple ($75K combined) with all obligor delinquencies already in-flight; BRKR.IO's Venus Brands single tranche of $132K is 26 days past partner due.

Charge-offs total $4.63M over the trailing twelve months, with Koverly accounting for 37% at $1.73M despite currently clean delinquency metrics, suggesting historical portfolio quality issues now resolved. Avalara and Order together represent $2.28M, followed by Flychain at $459K. Lawtrades, BRKR.IO, and smaller partners account for $153K combined. No distinct pattern emerges by sector or obligor type; charge-off timing suggests seasoned losses rather than current-period stress acceleration.

Loss % by origination cohort — cumulative losses ÷ originated ($K stacked bars, loss % line)

Bars = originated ($K) per cohort month. Lines on right axis = % of that cohort’s originated balance currently 30–60 / 60–90 / 90–120 DPD (amber/orange/red) and cumulative loss % (black). Recent cohorts understated as losses accrue over time.

Loss by underwriting vintage — initial line assigned vs 120+ DPD balance as % of line

X-axis = month of initial underwrite decision. Bars = total credit line assigned at first underwrite ($K). Red line = cumulative loss % of line assigned (right axis). Recent vintages understated as losses take time to develop.

Loss by underwriting vintage — total borrowed vs 120+ DPD balance as % of total borrowed

X-axis = month of initial underwrite decision. Bars = total amount actually borrowed (sum of original_amount across all loans) by orgs in each vintage cohort ($K). Red line = cumulative 120+ DPD balance as % of total borrowed (right axis). Denominator is realized borrowing, not credit line capacity. Recent vintages understated.

Loss ratio by origination cohort — cumulative 120+ DPD losses ÷ cumulative income collected

Bars = income collected from each cohort’s loans ($K): interest + fees + late fees + non-accrual items. Red line = cumulative loss % of cumulative income (right axis). Dashed orange line = monthly loss ratio (non-cumulative). Toggle partners to include/exclude from aggregate. Recent cohorts understated as both losses and income continue to accrue.

OatFi Credit Risk  ·  DPD methodology: cutoffdate − mat_dt, curr_upb > 0. Leading indicator: past partner due_date, not yet past OatFi mat_dt. Loss rate: first 120 DPD crossing or chargeoff ÷ trailing 12m originations × 12. Utilization >120% excluded. Partners and orgs under $10K excluded.
